refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a key performance indicator (KPI) requirement that directly impacts customer productivity and system acceptance. Refinement adds explicit reference to measurement/monitoring (telemetry) and duty cycle context to make verification concrete. High safety criticality tailoring requires that cycle time optimization does not compromise safety interlocks or emergency response.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Instrumented field trials under representative duty cycle; p95 cycle time computed from telemetry logs; comparison against CT_target acceptance threshold; safety event correlation analysis to ensure no safety compromise.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within design envelope.
FR-NGHE-002.2: The NGHE solution shall collect and transmit cycle time telemetry (start, end, sub-phase timestamps) to enable p95 KPI calculation.
FR-NGHE-002.3: The NGHE solution shall not sacrifice safety-critical response times (e.g., emergency stop, load stability checks) in pursuit of cycle time targets.
FR-NGHE-002.4: The teleoperation subsystem (if active) shall not introduce latency that degrades cycle time below CT_target.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Optimize vehicle powertrain (engine, transmission, hydraulics) for peak efficiency and responsiveness; minimize parasitic losses; tune load-travel-dump sequence timing.

DP-B: Implement real-time cycle time monitoring and adaptive control logic that adjusts vehicle parameters (throttle, gear selection, bucket speed) to maintain p95 ≤ CT_target while respecting safety constraints.
DP-C: Integrate teleoperation with low-latency network link and predictive control algorithms to reduce operator reaction time and improve cycle consistency.
DP-D: Establish a closed-loop performance feedback system: telemetry → KPI calculation → operator/site manager dashboard → operational tuning recommendations.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B + DP-D (Integrated approach): Implement real-time cycle time monitoring with adaptive vehicle control logic (DP-B) coupled to a closed-loop performance feedback system (DP-D) that enables continuous KPI tracking, operator awareness, and site-level tuning. This approach directly addresses the KPI requirement while maintaining safety criticality via explicit constraint enforcement.

potential_sources: Coupling between vehicle control (DP-B) and safety services: adaptive control must not override safety interlocks. Mitigation: safety constraints are hard-coded as non-negotiable limits in control logic.

Coupling between telemetry collection (DP-D) and network reliability: KPI feedback depends on reliable data transmission. Mitigation: telemetry buffering and retry logic; graceful degradation if network unavailable.

Coupling between teleoperation latency (DP-C, if invoked) and cycle time target: remote operation may increase variance. Mitigation: teleoperation is optional; local autonomous/semi-autonomous modes are primary for cycle time KPI.
coupling_check.notes: Selected DP avoids over-coupling by keeping vehicle powertrain optimization (DP-A) as a separate design concern (delegated to vehicle subsystem design). The selected DP focuses on monitoring, feedback, and adaptive control—the system-level levers for KPI achievement.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Vehicle powertrain is capable of achieving CT_target under nominal conditions; DP-B/D optimize scheduling and feedback, not fundamental capability.

Risk: Adaptive control logic may introduce unintended oscillations or instability if tuning parameters are not carefully validated. Mitigation: extensive simulation and field validation before deployment.
Risk: Telemetry latency or data loss could degrade KPI feedback accuracy. Mitigation: redundant telemetry paths; local cycle time estimation as fallback.

Risk: Site conditions (terrain, load variability, operator skill) may cause high variance in cycle time; p95 target may be unachievable without operational discipline. Mitigation: site-level acceptance criteria negotiation; operator training.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify CT_target value and duty cycle definition (load mass, travel distance, terrain type, ambient conditions) as part of system requirements baseline. Specify acceptable variance in cycle time (e.g., p5, p50, p95 percentiles) to guide control tuning. Define safety constraint boundaries (e.g., minimum load stability margin, maximum bucket speed) that adaptive control must respect.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: System-level integration module responsible for orchestrating cycle time KPI achievement across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services. Owns the real-time monitoring logic, adaptive control coordination, and closed-loop feedback system. Interfaces with Vehicle for control commands and telemetry; with Teleoperation for operator input and latency management; with Safety Services for constraint enforcement.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits cycle time telemetry (phase timestamps, cycle duration, sub-phase metrics) to external monitoring/analytics systems. Enables p95 KPI calculation and operator dashboard.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives operational tasking (load location, dump location, cycle parameters) from site manager or autonomous planning layer. Tasking includes CT_target and duty cycle context.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives safety constraints and alerts from Safety Services (e.g., load stability limits, emergency stop signals). Emits cycle time performance data to Safety Services for correlation with safety events.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses Network Link for telemetry transmission, tasking reception, and (if teleoperation active) low-latency control signaling. Network reliability directly impacts KPI feedback and teleoperation cycle time.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural system-level owner of cycle time KPI orchestration. Monitoring, adaptive control, and feedback logic are core to the solution's value proposition. Minimal diffusion: only telemetry emission and tasking reception cross boundaries.

Vehicle owns powertrain and mechanical execution (load, travel, dump). However, cycle time KPI is a system-level metric that requires coordination across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety. If cycle time logic is embedded in Vehicle, it becomes tightly coupled to vehicle firmware and difficult to tune at system level. Not recommended as primary owner.

Teleoperation is responsible for remote operator interface and low-latency control. Cycle time KPI is not primarily a teleoperation concern; it is a system-level performance metric. Assigning cycle time logic to Teleoperation would create diffusion (logic scattered across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety). Not recommended.

Safety Services owns safety constraints and emergency response. While cycle time optimization must respect safety limits, the primary responsibility for KPI achievement is system-level, not safety-specific. Assigning cycle time logic to Safety Services would blur safety and performance concerns. Not recommended as primary owner.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner because: (1) Cycle time KPI is a system-level performance metric that requires orchestration across multiple subsystems (vehicle, teleoperation, safety). (2) Real-time monitoring and adaptive control logic are best centralized at the system level to avoid diffusion and enable consistent tuning. (3) Closed-loop feedback (telemetry → KPI → operator awareness → tuning) is a system-level concern. (4) NGHE Solution already owns the integration layer and has visibility into all subsystem states.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution becomes overloaded with control logic, it may become a bottleneck or single point of failure. Mitigation: decompose cycle time logic into well-defined sub-modules (monitoring, adaptive control, feedback) with clear interfaces; consider distributed implementation if performance demands warrant.

Risk: Tight coupling between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation/Safety Services could make the system brittle. Mitigation: enforce strict interface contracts; use event-driven or message-based communication to decouple logic.

Risk: Telemetry and Network Link reliability directly impact KPI feedback. If network is unavailable, KPI calculation is delayed or incomplete. Mitigation: implement local cycle time estimation as fallback; buffer telemetry for later transmission; design graceful degradation.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define NGHE Solution's internal architecture to separate concerns: (1) Cycle Time Monitor (collects phase timestamps, computes cycle metrics), (2) Adaptive Control Coordinator (adjusts vehicle parameters within safety constraints), (3) KPI Feedback Engine (calculates p95, emits telemetry, updates operator dashboard). Each sub-module should have clear ownership and interfaces. Specify the contract between NGHE Solution and Vehicle (e.g., control command format, telemetry schema) to enable independent development and testing.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (real-time monitoring + adaptive control + closed-loop feedback) directly addresses the refined FR (achieve p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target with monitoring and safety constraints). DP is solution-neutral (does not prescribe specific algorithms or hardware) and allows multiple implementation paths. Coupling check passes: safety constraints are explicitly enforced as hard limits in control logic.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion risk: If cycle time logic is scattered across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services instead of centralized in NGHE Solution, tuning and maintenance become difficult. Mitigation: NGHE Solution is the clear owner; Vehicle/Teleoperation/Safety Services provide data and constraint inputs only.

Diffusion risk: Telemetry collection and transmission depend on Network Link reliability. If telemetry is not reliably delivered, KPI feedback is incomplete. Mitigation: implement local buffering and retry logic; design fallback mechanisms.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution achieves 95% containment score for cycle time KPI logic. External interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and minimal. Ownership is clear: NGHE Solution orchestrates; Vehicle/Teleoperation/Safety Services provide inputs and constraints.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible via instrumented field trials: (1) Collect cycle time telemetry under representative duty cycle. (2) Compute p95 from telemetry logs. (3) Compare against CT_target. (4) Correlate with safety events to ensure no safety compromise. (5) Validate adaptive control tuning via simulation and controlled testing.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns cycle time KPI achievement; Vehicle owns powertrain execution; Teleoperation owns remote operator interface; Safety Services owns safety constraints. Each module has a well-defined role and interface.

recommended_revisions: Refine CT_target and duty cycle definition (load mass, travel distance, terrain, ambient conditions) as part of system requirements baseline. This is critical for DP tuning and verification.
Specify acceptable variance in cycle time (p5, p50, p95 percentiles) to guide adaptive control tuning and acceptance criteria.
Define safety constraint boundaries (e.g., minimum load stability margin, maximum bucket speed) that adaptive control must respect. These are hard limits, not soft targets.
Establish telemetry schema and Network Link reliability requirements to ensure KPI feedback is accurate and timely.
Plan for operator training and site-level operational discipline to minimize cycle time variance due to human factors.

decision.justification: The refined FR is not yet atomic; it contains four sub-FRs that require further decomposition. The selected DP (real-time monitoring + adaptive control + closed-loop feedback) is sound at the system level, but each sub-FR requires its own DP-IM assignment to ensure complete coverage and clear ownership. Proceeding to the next decomposition level will clarify the detailed design and implementation strategy.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within design envelope.
FR-NGHE-002.2: The NGHE solution shall collect and transmit cycle time telemetry (start, end, sub-phase timestamps) to enable p95 KPI calculation.
FR-NGHE-002.3: The NGHE solution shall not sacrifice safety-critical response times (e.g., emergency stop, load stability checks) in pursuit of cycle time targets.
FR-NGHE-002.4: The teleoperation subsystem (if active) shall not introduce latency that degrades cycle time below CT_target.

assumptions: Vehicle powertrain is capable of achieving CT_target under nominal conditions; DP-B/D optimize scheduling and feedback, not fundamental capability.
CT_target and duty cycle are well-defined and achievable under site conditions.
Network Link is sufficiently reliable to support telemetry transmission and (if applicable) teleoperation control.
Safety constraints (load stability, emergency response) are non-negotiable and will not be compromised by cycle time optimization.
Operator training and site-level operational discipline are in place to minimize cycle time variance due to human factors.
open_risks: Adaptive control logic may introduce unintended oscillations or instability if tuning parameters are not carefully validated.
Telemetry latency or data loss could degrade KPI feedback accuracy.
Site conditions (terrain, load variability, operator skill) may cause high variance in cycle time; p95 target may be unachievable without operational discipline.
Teleoperation latency (if active) may increase cycle time variance and degrade KPI achievement.
NGHE Solution may become overloaded with control logic if not carefully decomposed into sub-modules.
evidence_needed: CT_target value and duty cycle definition (load mass, travel distance, terrain type, ambient conditions).
Acceptable variance in cycle time (p5, p50, p95 percentiles) for tuning and acceptance criteria.
Safety constraint boundaries (e.g., minimum load stability margin, maximum bucket speed) that adaptive control must respect.
Telemetry schema and Network Link reliability requirements.
Simulation and field validation results for adaptive control tuning.
Operator training plan and site-level operational procedures.
